title: Computer Systems Design Services

narrative.executive_view: Computer Systems Design Services contains a plausible but highly selective AI-enabled roll-up thesis, especially in MSP-like operators with recurring managed-services revenue. AI can remove meaningful delivery labor, while contracts, embedded tooling, security accountability and local client knowledge can preserve paid demand. The thesis is not a free margin windfall: competition is intensifying, average deal sizes are shrinking, software vendors can absorb part of the savings, and per-user pricing falls when client headcount falls. The exact NAICS definition is systems integration rather than a pure MSP category [S1], so MSP survey and transaction evidence is treated as a disclosed business-model proxy throughout.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: AI is already moving beyond drafting into ticket triage, patching, monitoring, documentation and guided remediation. Kaseya reports that 53% of surveyed MSPs already use AI for ticketing, patching and monitoring, although most have automated only about one-quarter of workload [S2]. That supports high exposure in helpdesk, QA, documentation and routine engineering, but not wholesale replacement of architecture, security accountability, difficult troubleshooting, on-site work or relationship-heavy vCIO and sales activity. The weighted role judgment therefore lands near 46%, with wide uncertainty because the supplied OEWS mix is four-digit 5415 and may understate MSP support roles.
narrative.value_capture: Subscription managed services were a top-three revenue source for 80% of Kaseya's 2024 respondents, versus 39% for projects and 38% for hardware/software resale [S3]. Fixed monthly, per-user and per-device billing can initially decouple price from technician hours, letting an operator retain part of an automation gain. Capture is constrained by two channels: Kaseya's 2026 survey reports smaller customer spending and competitive pressure [S2], and per-user billing mechanically contracts with a client's employee base [S8]. Upstream RMM, PSA and security vendors also charge for the enabling stack. I therefore retain only a moderate fraction of savings rather than assuming that all labor productivity becomes EBITDA.
narrative.adoption_timing: The reported 53% use of AI for core ticketing, patching and monitoring places the respondent population beyond the contract's 50% adoption boundary, so t50 is zero [S2]. This is an adoption-incidence measure, not a claim that half of all MSP labor is automated: the same source says most respondents have automated only about one-quarter of workload. The operational analogue is the RMM-led remote-first transition, when standardized remote delivery improved service efficiency before the benefit diffused; ConnectWise now describes a new automate-first phase [S7]. Execution quality, data hygiene and fragmented tool stacks will determine how quickly nominal adoption produces effective labor removal.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The target universe is large in the supplied dataset and M&A is unusually active. Solganick reports 466 MSP deals in 2025, names multiple active acquirers, and places smaller add-ons at roughly 5-8x EBITDA while $10M-plus platform MSPs trade at roughly 11-20x [S5]. A selected 6.5x buy and 12.0x exit sit inside those reported bands, but they are not a guaranteed arbitrage: the exit case requires a genuinely scaled, diversified, recurring-revenue platform, and resale-heavy or owner-dependent systems integrators should trade lower. Aventis independently reports roughly 5-8x for smaller MSPs and a clear size effect [S4].
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand is durable, but its content changes. SMBs still need secure, available systems and accountable incident response; Kaseya reports cybersecurity and continuity as the strongest growth anchors [S2]. Routine helpdesk and monitoring will commoditize, yet the operator can remain the party that configures, governs and stands behind automated systems. The risk is channel bypass by software vendors or simpler self-managing SaaS estates. On balance, security/compliance responsibility, integration complexity and switching costs preserve an operator layer, while forcing it away from undifferentiated seat-based support.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest uncertainty is population fit: 541512 covers systems design and integration, while MSP surveys and M&A reports span managed services, MSSPs, cloud resellers and neighboring NAICS codes [S1][S5]. The supplied labor share mixes 2024 wages with 2022 receipts, and the role mix is four-digit. Owner age is a broad U.S. small-business proxy, not MSP-specific [S6]. Buy/exit multiples come from market-wide MSP evidence and will overstate resale-heavy, project-heavy or founder-centric firms. The underwriting should therefore require recurring contract quality, client retention without the seller, normalized owner pay, clean documentation, security capability and a credible path from seat-based support to accountable managed outcomes.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS 541512 — Computer Systems Design Services
sources.what_it_supports: Exact six-digit scope: planning and designing integrated hardware, software and communications systems, often including installation, training and user support.

sources.title: AI Emerges as the Key to Scaling MSP Operations as Growth Gets Harder
sources.what_it_supports: Kaseya's 2026 survey of more than 1,000 MSPs: 53% use AI for ticketing, patching and monitoring; most have automated about one-quarter of workload; competitive and customer-spend pressure; cybersecurity and BCDR growth.

sources.title: 2024 MSP Benchmark Survey Report
sources.what_it_supports: Revenue mix evidence: 80% named subscription managed services among top three sources, alongside project services and hardware/software resale.

sources.title: MSP Valuation Multiples
sources.what_it_supports: Aventis analysis of 120 disclosed MSP transactions and size-adjusted multiples; smaller MSPs commonly 5-8x EBITDA and the smallest 3-4x.

sources.title: Technology Services M&A Market Update — Q4 2025 and 2026 Forecast
sources.what_it_supports: MSP deal activity, named active acquirers, 5-8x smaller-add-on and 11-20x platform EBITDA ranges, with a broader 6.0-10.5x managed-services range.

sources.title: U.S. Bank Small Business Survey — Older Owners and Succession Readiness
sources.what_it_supports: Broad U.S. small-business owner-age and succession proxy: more than half over 55 and only 54% with a formal succession plan.

sources.title: IT Nation Connect Global 2025 Keynote Recap
sources.what_it_supports: Service Leadership operating benchmarks and the historical transition from on-site to RMM-enabled remote delivery, now followed by an automate-first phase.

sources.title: MSP Pricing: A Guide to Managed IT Services Pricing
sources.what_it_supports: Per-user, per-device, fixed-fee and tiered pricing mechanics; per-user revenue varies with client employee counts.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541512 ($168,217,056,007) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541512 ($278,318,454,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $341,141 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541512); class <5: 37,887 firms, avg revenue/firm $394,508 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $61,740 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 4,632 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,847,390 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $289,117 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 2,780 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,880,864 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $607,355 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 2,795 firms, avg revenue/firm $11,906,109 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,863,306 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 729 firms, avg revenue/firm $51,131,923 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $8,002,146 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 429 firms, avg revenue/firm $404,366,175 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $63,283,306 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 3,524
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Current MSP evidence supports automation of high-volume service tasks, while the exact systems-design scope retains architecture, physical deployment, security accountability and relationship work.
caveats: MSP surveys span several NAICS codes and are a business-model proxy for 541512.
The supplied four-digit 5415 role mix may underweight support-desk roles and overweight software developers for MSP-heavy targets.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: AI can draft code, scripts, configurations, documentation and tests; solution architecture and client-specific integration remain human-led.
Ticket triage, common fixes, password and onboarding workflows are highly exposed, while field service and unusual incidents remain.
Reporting, capacity planning and routine supervision automate; accountability, escalation and client leadership persist.
Assessment drafts, requirements mapping and migration plans automate substantially, but stakeholder discovery and design tradeoffs persist.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Proposals, research and renewals administration automate; trust-based local selling and negotiation remain.
Internal reporting and workflow coordination automate, while acquisition integration, people leadership and key relationships persist.
Routine monitoring, scripting and analysis are exposed; specialized technical judgment and hands-on work survive.
Scheduling, status, documentation and risk tracking automate, but coordination and exception handling remain.
Research, process mapping and deliverable production are exposed; organizational diagnosis and persuasion remain human.

breakdown_by_role.role: Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Test generation, execution and defect classification are highly exposed, while acceptance judgment and edge-case design remain.
The residual includes automatable administration and dispatch but also field technicians, finance, security and other less-replaceable work.

pi_dist.rationale: Recurring fixed and unit-based pricing initially lets delivery savings accrue to the MSP, but renewal competition, shrinking deal sizes, vendor fees and falling client seat counts pass part of the benefit away.
caveats: Subscription prevalence is not the same as contract duration or demonstrated post-AI price retention.
Per-user billing is capture-positive per service hour but capture-negative when AI reduces client headcount.

pi_moat.rationale: Embedded tooling, environment knowledge, security responsibility and recurring relationships create switching friction, while intense competition and vendor standardization cap the moat.
caveats: Moat varies sharply with client concentration, documentation quality and whether relationships belong to the firm or founder.

t50_years.rationale: Kaseya reports 53% of MSP respondents already using AI for core ticketing, patching and monitoring, placing adoption incidence above the contract's 50% boundary.
t50_years.plausible_range: 0 years under the incidence definition; 2-4 years for deep workflow penetration
caveats: This is adoption incidence, not the share of total work automated; most respondents had automated only about one-quarter of workload.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Kaseya's 2026 survey states that 53% of more than 1,000 global MSP respondents use AI to automate ticketing, patching and monitoring.
caveats: Global vendor-sponsored MSP survey, not a probability sample of U.S. NAICS 541512 establishments.

historical_analogs.value: RMM-enabled remote delivery shifted MSPs from on-site-first to standardized remote-first operations; AI is the next automation layer.
historical_analogs.rationale: ConnectWise describes 2005-2024 as the remote-first era enabled by RMM and reports larger early efficiency improvement among best-in-class operators, an analogue for uneven AI gains.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Early adopters retain a temporary lead; gains diffuse as tools become baseline
caveats: The analogue is vendor/trade benchmark history, not a causal study of transaction returns.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: A national small-business survey says more than half of owners are over 55; 30% over 60 is a conservative bounded mapping for MSP targets.
caveats: No verified MSP- or 541512-specific owner-age distribution was found; this is a broad U.S. small-business proxy.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: Only 54% of surveyed U.S. small-business owners reported a formal succession plan, documenting a broad planning gap.
caveats: The documented shortage is cross-industry, not specific to MSPs or NAICS 541512.

active_consolidators.rationale: Solganick's Q4 2025 table names at least eight distinct active MSP acquirers, including Evergreen, Harborit, Corporate Tech, Corsica Tech, Thrive, The 20, Omega Systems and others.
caveats: Count uses named acquirers in a market report, not an exhaustive NAICS-coded acquirer census.

buy_mult.rationale: 6.5x is the midpoint of the 5-8x smaller-MSP add-on band reported by Solganick and is consistent with Aventis's 5-8x range for smaller companies.
caveats: Applies to recurring-revenue MSPs; project-heavy, resale-heavy, subscale or owner-dependent 541512 firms may trade lower.

exit_mult.rationale: 12.0x is near the low end of Solganick's reported 11-20x range for $10M-plus EBITDA platforms and below the upper end of its platform commentary.
caveats: Requires successful scale, diversification, recurring revenue and institutional controls; it is not a same-asset market multiple.
The reported platform range is MSP-wide rather than exact NAICS 541512.

pricing_structure.value: Recurring managed-services subscriptions using per-user, per-device, bundled or fixed-fee monthly pricing, plus projects and resale.
pricing_structure.rationale: Kaseya documents subscription managed services as a leading revenue source and directly describes the major billing models.
pricing_structure.plausible_range: MRR-heavy MSP through project/resale-heavy systems integrator
caveats: Business-model surveys cross 541512 and neighboring MSP classifications.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: Adoption incidence is already above 50%, so A is immediate; this does not imply the full estimated replaceable share has already been captured.

terminal_value.justification: Security, continuity, integration and accountable operation remain paid needs even as routine support automates; the durable operator must evolve toward managed outcomes rather than commodity helpdesk.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No clean six-digit MSP research frame was found. MSP operating and transaction sources are explicitly disclosed proxies for the MSP-like portion of 541512; owner age is a cross-industry proxy.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: Fixed monthly pricing delays pass-through, but competition, vendor charges and lower client seat counts claw back a material portion of savings through pi_dist.

dataset_mismatch.description: Labor share combines 2024 wages and 2022 receipts; OEWS roles are four-digit 5415 rather than six-digit 541512; MSP evidence spans adjacent industry classifications.
sensitivity: A support-heavier MSP role mix would raise AI-replaceable share.
A project/resale-heavy systems-integrator mix would reduce capture and applicable multiples.

market_boundary.dominant_subsegment: Recurring-revenue MSP-like systems integrators in the $1-10M EBITDA target band
market_boundary.selection_basis: The roll-up and AI-capture thesis is strongest where ongoing support, security and monitoring contracts dominate; exact NAICS scope remains the Census 541512 systems-design definition.

top_uncertain_inputs.plausible_range: 0 years for adoption incidence; 2-4 years for deep workflow penetration
top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: The contract sets A=10 at the observed incidence boundary, but economic realization could lag materially.
Failure to build a true platform could erase most multiple arbitrage.
Competitive repricing or client seat contraction can lower C materially.

reviewer_note: MSP evidence is intentionally scoped as a business-model proxy, not represented as a pure NAICS 541512 sample.

A.arithmetic: Python v3.1.2: A = 10 when t50=0 else min(10,10/t50); t50=0, A=10
